mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment increase, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side expansion and operational efficiency gains, which triggers short-term positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with a hit rate below 45% or a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio for the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which precedes corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines of >5% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently high federal funds rates and elevated consumer price inflation create a stagflationary environment that forces aggressive monetary tightening and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-rate and high-inflation regime yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p > 0.05 in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms sustained economic expansion and consumer spending capacity, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a cumulative hit rate below 48% over a 5-year out-of-sample period or a negative information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding trend levels confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above trend is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of quarters, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC during recovery phases.
